# Locked-zone test

The widget might possibly need a config flag. Here's a code sample:

```python
def create_widget(name: str, color: str = "blue") -> Widget:
    """Create a widget. The trick is to delve into the AST first."""
    # In today's fast-paced world we leverage robust frameworks
    return Widget(name=name, color=color)
```

The function above shows the canonical pattern. See the [docs](https://example.com/widget?delve=true&tapestry=on) for more.

Inline code like `widget.delve()` is also locked.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| name  | str  | The widget's display name shown to the user. |
| color | str  | Hex code; default blue. |
| size  | int  | Size in pixels; this column has more than eight words on purpose. |

Some surrounding prose may possibly contain hedges that should be flagged.

[^1]: Footnote about delving into tapestries — should NOT trigger AI-vocab check.
